_comment: SUPERSEDED 2026-06-30: game/coords.py now derives hex<->pixel<->global-axial from the EXACT VASSAL grid formula (data/cna_map_grid.json), not from these fits. Retained as historical reference; clean_points_map_c are still handy as ground-truth town pixels. Empirical calibration of the CNA VASSAL map hex lattice (map: CNA Map Vassal Mitch Guthrie 2021.png, 14310x4632), derived by detecting town-dot pixels for rules-confirmed hex labels and fitting.

convention.orientation: flat-top hexes in vertical columns (VASSAL sideways=true)
convention.offset_to_axial: col=XX (first two digits) is the staggering axis; even-q offset. r = YY - (XX + (XX & 1)) / 2; q = XX.
convention.note: This convention was the clear winner in a linear-fit search (max residual 34px vs 56-87px for col=YY / odd-q). Hex neighbours follow exactly from the axial conversion and do NOT depend on pixel precision.
convention.axes: XX (1st 2 digits) ~ north-south (higher XX = north); YY (2nd 2 digits) ~ east-west (higher YY = east). Confirmed vs map: Tobruk C4807 is coastal/north of El Adem C4507; Bardia C4321 is east of Tobruk.

map_c_fit._note: px,py = f(q,r) linear fit, Map C only, ~25px mean residual (dot-detection noise). Refine with cleaner dot detection before using for terrain sampling.

todo: Robustify dot detection (exact town-dot colour + roundness) to drop residual to <10px for terrain-grade pixel mapping.

Establish cross-section continuity (global axial across Maps A-E): either derive VASSAL's exact numbering formula from the per-section hOff/vOff/vDescend params, or calibrate one reference town per section.
Build game/coords.py (label<->axial<->pixel + neighbours) on this convention and validate.
